item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This site will never stop surprising me, really. It amazes me every day. Upvoted.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that the image used in this article was given to the author with permission by Arlen Markob, and thus is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After looking through this again, I realized something depressingly funny.

These kids literally have no future.

irrelevant, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hm, I don't think I quite understand this.

WMG?
– hide block

Re-reading it, I got the idea that the kids were going to get shot by some man, and to save them another person made them stuck at the moment just before the bullet hits; so they may want to get out, but that would kill them (and that'd be the reason research got discontinued).


However, that seems more like a wild-mass-guess rather than an interpretation actually supported by the text. No-voting for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Didn't hit me in the feels. Or in the curious. Not sure why, not feeling it.

The whole problem is, there's no possibility in it.

Does everyone get the same dialogue?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The writing is a little rough, and not everything is as clear as it should be (this is not an SCP where mystery is important to the concept), but man…this hits me in the feels. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What can I say? This wasn't fun to read. The Description is vague on what actually occurs, the kids' dialogue is hard to read, and the chrono-babble reminds me of every wanky argument over time travel grammar I've seen in fiction, played completely seriously.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Deliciously creepy. What I particularly like is that the two children are aware of being trapped in time…
Just curious: IS the shooter the kids mention Agent Ueda or not? I did not understand that it is. For me, the shooting incident (in everyone's past, but the children's frozen present) started the time loop through some trauma, or a strong wish not to die.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reading this again, I couldn't help but be reminded of these two. My headcanon now dictates that these two are them as children in an alternate timeline where the botched murder attempt caused this to happen instead of being displaced throughout all time and space.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one a lot, the impression I get is it's like a "haunting," but it's not because they're the lost spirits of dead kids; they're somehow trapped in multiple chronological states at once. Maybe they fell outside the normal continuity of time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Screwing with time again. I love it, although it seems a bit lacking of any real explanation aside from this thing where these people appear and are all timefucked. Creepy, sad, and confusing-in-a-good-way enough for an upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had thought the same too, that the entities experienced that possibility but the gender of the shooter and the Agent don't match.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Another little beautiful thing, by Soulless to the world. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At one point they were talking about someone who is/was/will shoot(ing) them, which made me think the agent who pulled his gun out earlier would shoot them by the end.
But that didn't happen, which really threw me for a loop.

Are you sure you don't get it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Soulless, this is a horrible thing to do to verb tenses. I love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There isn't really a does. It's just an "is" without any "will be".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Thoughts
-

Also, I'm not quite sure what's going on with this.
At one point they were talking about someone who is/was/will shoot(ing) them, which made me think the agent who pulled his gun out earlier would shoot them by the end.
But that didn't happen, which really threw me for a loop.
And then it ended with me not being any more the wiser about what this does.
So, neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You seem to have described SCP-2909-A as two different things. I'm certain you meant the second one to be -B.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to wonder what happened for research to stop so suddenly…

+1

item.title: Re: Am vs Will Be vs Has Been
item.content:encoded: If you wanna know some nice online resources for peer-review sites and such, hit me up on a PM.

item.title: Re: Am vs Will Be vs Has Been
item.content:encoded: Ok. If/when I finish writing my serial, I'll find my own venue. Good luck on getting published, man!

item.title: Re: Am vs Will Be vs Has Been
item.content:encoded: It's a novella that probably is going to be worked on in order to be actually published, as in getting paid for it, and will therefore not be put online unless for peer review on other sites for that kind of thing. It's unrelated to the Foundation, so it's probably not gonna show up here at all, heh…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Tragic and deftly executed, but a little too brief for my tastes. Novote, bordering on an upvote.
E: After giving this some thought, I've changed my mind. Heartily upvoted.

item.title: Re: Am vs Will Be vs Has Been
item.content:encoded: Novella, you say? How do I shot online publishing?

item.title: Re: Am vs Will Be vs Has Been
item.content:encoded: Basically this. NaNoWriMo (National Novel Writing Month) is a challenge to write 50k words in the month of november. It's originally meant to be to help people stop worrying about editing and just write their novel, but I'm using it to push out all my SCP ideas. After I run out of ideas, I'm just gonna work on my novella.

item.title: Re: Am vs Will Be vs Has Been
item.content:encoded:
November is National Novel Writing Month.

Here you go.

item.title: Re: Am vs Will Be vs Has Been
item.content:encoded:
Ok, I keep seeing mentions of it, so I have to ask: what is NaNoWriMo? Is it a challenge? A user who's making requests? A genre/subgenre of fiction? What?

+1, creepy skip, no matter what the answer is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is eerie. Good job. I love sentences that don't conform to one tense in order to convey a temporal issue. (As specific as that sounds. :P)

item.title: Am vs Will Be vs Has Been
item.content:encoded:
Some people feel the world has left them behind. Everyone else has moved on while they are stuck still.

For NaNoWriMo.

Feel free to consider this a continuation of "Soulless tries to kick it old school".
